response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack software engineer with a strong web and application development background spanning over 20 years, but his profile has virtually no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, and GPU/SIMD architecture — none of which appear in his resume. His AI-adjacent work is limited to agentic workflow tooling and MCP frameworks, which is application-layer software development, not hardware or systems-level work. While his low-latency work at Sociomantic and embedded systems mentions are minor positives, they do not approach the hardware optimization depth this principal-level role demands.
strengths: 20+ years of continuous professional software engineering experience, meeting the BS+8yr qualification bar on tenure alone
Low-latency and performance-conscious development experience at Sociomantic using async I/O, caching, and high-throughput systems
Breadth of systems exposure including embedded, mobile, and infrastructure work, showing adaptability across domains
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience whatsoever — the core technical requirement of the role is entirely absent
No hardware-software co-design, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator experience of any kind
Deep learning expertise is absent; AI involvement is limited to application-layer agent tooling unrelated to DNN training or inference optimization

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware background
No AI accelerator or GPU workload experience
Long tenure and performance-aware engineering history
